en_body:
John sat in silence, a glass of wine in his hand, his cigarette forgotten in the ashtray. I was silent too. The scented candle swayed languorously, sweet gentle vanilla breathing through the room. Incense-silence released its circling breath, mingling with the smoke from the cigarette in my hand and John's forgotten cigarette, drifting slowly into the air. Louis Armstrong's ghostly horn coiled into the amorous river of jazz, and the urgent drums beat accompaniment for the year's last night as it slowly passed into the Past, waiting upon the Future! John looked up; I smiled softly.

--Perhaps it is always so, isn't it, Viola? Those who have the power to bestow are always more hesitant
 to give than those who are supplicating?!
--The Season of Christmas has only just passed, John! (smiling)
--Human beings are always less magnanimous than the Divine Beings!
--And human beings are always filled with limitations, are we not? Therefore do we not always have
 the need to turn our gaze toward the Grace of Salvation and Transcendence beyond all that is within us?
--Can we not learn the gift of giving from the Most-Holy One?
--Yes! But there are things we have already offered away, and no longer possess to give
 once again..!!

 John lowered his head, his face as if wilted with sorrow! Janis Joplin's hoarse dark voice moaned and filled the little room. He asked me softly, as if still striving to catch one last Hope:

--May I wait longer for Viola? Or does Viola truly know her own heart?
--John! No one can give you that answer except yourself! As with me!
--Why? This pursuit of Love—is it like this?
--With me, John? There are no pursuits, John! There is only an experiencing
 until something we have always waited for suddenly brings an ultimate and infinite Meaning
 within us, then rises full, wholly enclosing all the fullness of that waiting: and that is the first
 Revelation, utterly complete: and once it is Revelation, there is nothing left for us to deny..!!
--Is Viola too self-assured? How can a creature filled with limitations be certain
 she has attained something eternal like the Revelation of Love? Until our eyes close,
 perhaps we shall not understand anything in the fullness of its Meaning! Does not everyone
 know that no one has ever revealed the secret of the realm of Life hidden within Death?

--Let me give John this consummate line of Vietnamese poetry, this line of Love: "Beyond
 the veranda, the Tử tree has grown to an embrace." I cannot answer for anyone else: but as for
 me, within my own rather too-embracing limitations: from those limitations, I contem-
 plate the infinite and most Absolute thirst within me: to understand when a thirst
 suddenly seems filled beyond all measure: miraculously, because it does not
 forever overflow, but whispers itself empty to fill, fills then empties: so the thirst now pierces deep, now
 urges more fiercely: that is when I am satisfied..!! Satisfaction calls forth ever more vast
 and all-embracing searches! The satisfaction of one search, within that first longing:
 opens the circle of vision and the boundless reach of searching for all that remains..!!
 Please understand me, John: I hope I have not been too very obscure..!!

--You mean Viola has slaked the first thirst...and seeks the Peace to embark upon
 the far-voyage of her human lifetime?

--Yes! Through one human lifetime, I have thirsted for so much and searched forever, yet no
 answer could offer even the most limited satisfaction, because each lacked
 the power to contain the indefinite Aspiration: and when I felt the Love of That-One
 "inspires" within me: the vagueness of my own being ended. That full stop, that first
 ending of the first answer: allows me to open the colon beginning
 the far-voyage into the heart of Humanity, and to seek the necessary answers to
 our common questions, which yet are very particularly my own..!!

--Viola does not think what I feel for her is Love?
--I cannot answer that question for John! But I can share this feeling:
 Revelations are the Meaning that makes us feel the existential Being fits
 closely enough through our own very own perception within it..!!
--Even so, Viola gives me some Hope that what Viola calls the close
 and perfect embrace of existential Being—what Viola calls her first Revelation:
 I shall find it: at least I know I have been a witness that it has become
 Real for Viola!

 I went to John, knelt near him, lifted John's loosely fallen hand and spoke softly, with a longing smile of sincerity that might fully communicate the source of Compassion for a loss, so beginning the great labor of seeking Meaning:

--Beloved John! I thank you and give Thanks for the sincere feelings you hold for
 me alone! In a garden of ten-thousand crimson and thousand violet flowers, no blossom is less beautiful than
 those flowers blooming with all their heart, all their Love, offering themselves wholly into Earth and Heaven! But
 especially for those who seek the flower: there is singly one and only one blossom that can open
 wholly, perfectly, most closely into a One-And-Only-Flower-Season capable of making whole and fresh the heart of
 the seeker who longs to bind the Heart's Season..!! I think John should congratulate himself: for John is one
 among the rare precious hearts that know how to exalt the waiting, the longing search for the one
 Meaningful Flower-Season: the ultimate Meaning that is the Last within its own First...!
 Within the forever and ever limitations of the Relative: there are summits upon the Absolute of
 the Absolute..!! We must not compare the expansions and contractions of Absolute coordinates by the
 limitations we have broken through: we should only take them as Blessed-Vows..!! In one human life
 merely to feel it is already too great a Happiness..!!

 John lifted my hand, touched it to his lips, and one warm tear fell upon the back of my hand. Silently I understood the pain of a man I had refused.

--Viola! It hurts so so so badly..!!
note: The quoted line about the Tử tree alludes to Nguyễn Du's Truyện Kiều, where passing time is measured by a tree grown large enough to embrace.
